cards.frontJa: If it ___ tomorrow, I will stay home.
cards.backEn: If it rains tomorrow, I will stay home.

cards.frontJa: If the train ___ late, we'll miss the connection.
cards.backEn: If the train is late, we'll miss the connection.

cards.frontJa: If you ___ the file now, I'll review it tonight.
cards.backEn: If you send the file now, I'll review it tonight.

cards.frontJa: If you heat the soup, ___ it so it doesn't burn.
cards.backEn: If you heat the soup, stir it so it doesn't burn.

cards.frontJa: If there isn't enough room, we ___ use the other room.
cards.backEn: If there isn't enough room, we'll use the other room.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もし時間があれば、コーヒーでもどうですか。
cards.backEn: If you have time, how about a coffee?

cards.frontJa: Unless it stops raining, we can't ___ the match on time.
cards.backEn: Unless it stops raining, we can't start the match on time.

cards.frontJa: I'll call you if anything ___ .
cards.backEn: I'll call you if anything changes.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼が来なければ、始めましょう。
cards.backEn: If he doesn't come, let's start.

cards.frontJa: If you're not sure, ___ ask the staff.
cards.backEn: If you're not sure, just ask the staff.

cards.frontJa: We'll postpone the picnic if the wind ___ too strong.
cards.backEn: We'll postpone the picnic if the wind is too strong.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もし迷ったら、地図アプリを見てください。
cards.backEn: If you get lost, check the map app.

cards.frontJa: If you wait here, I ___ get the manager.
cards.backEn: If you wait here, I'll get the manager.

cards.frontJa: If you need a receipt, I ___ print one for you.
cards.backEn: If you need a receipt, I'll print one for you.

cards.frontJa: If it's cheaper online, we ___ order there.
cards.backEn: If it's cheaper online, we'll order there.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もしよければ、席をご案内します。
cards.backEn: If you like, I can show you to your seat.

cards.frontJa: If the meeting runs long, ___ take a taxi home.
cards.backEn: If the meeting runs long, I'll take a taxi home.

cards.frontJa: If you see Yuki, ___ her I'm running five minutes late.
cards.backEn: If you see Yuki, tell her I'm running five minutes late.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 雨が降ったら、室内でやりましょう。
cards.backEn: If it rains, let's do it indoors.

cards.frontJa: If there's no reply by Friday, ___ follow up by email.
cards.backEn: If there's no reply by Friday, I'll follow up by email.

cards.frontJa: If you ___ uncomfortable, say something right away.
cards.backEn: If you feel uncomfortable, say something right away.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 都合が悪ければ、別の日にしましょう。
cards.backEn: If it's not convenient, let's pick another day.

cards.frontJa: If we leave now, we ___ catch the last train.
cards.backEn: If we leave now, we'll catch the last train.

cards.frontJa: If the package arrives damaged, ___ contact support.
cards.backEn: If the package arrives damaged, please contact support.

cards.frontJa: ___ you finish the checklist, you can't board early.
cards.backEn: Unless you finish the checklist, you can't board early.

cards.frontJa: I'll attend ___ something urgent comes up.
cards.backEn: I'll attend unless something urgent comes up.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼が謝らない限り、許しません。
cards.backEn: I won't forgive him unless he apologizes.

cards.frontJa: You can use the lounge ___ you show your membership card.
cards.backEn: You can use the lounge provided you show your membership card.

cards.frontJa: We can ship today ___ the payment clears this afternoon.
cards.backEn: We can ship today provided the payment clears this afternoon.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 招待されない限り、パーティーには行きません。
cards.backEn: I won't go to the party unless I'm invited.

cards.frontJa: Unless we hear otherwise, we'll ___ at 9 a.m.
cards.backEn: Unless we hear otherwise, we'll start at 9 a.m.

cards.frontJa: I'll keep the seat ___ someone needs it more.
cards.backEn: I'll keep the seat unless someone needs it more.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 天気が良ければ、ハイキングに行きます。
cards.backEn: We'll go hiking as long as the weather is good.

cards.frontJa: You may leave early ___ your supervisor approves it.
cards.backEn: You may leave early provided your supervisor approves it.

cards.frontJa: Unless it's an emergency, ___ call after 10 p.m.
cards.backEn: Unless it's an emergency, don't call after 10 p.m.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 問題がなければ、明日発送します。
cards.backEn: We'll ship tomorrow as long as there are no issues.

cards.frontJa: ___ you repeat that more slowly?
cards.backEn: Could you repeat that more slowly?

cards.frontJa: ___ you send me the agenda by noon?
cards.backEn: Could you send me the agenda by noon?

cards.frontJa: ___ you hold the door for a second?
cards.backEn: Could you hold the door for a second?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 窓を少し開けていただけますか。
cards.backEn: Could you open the window a little?

cards.frontJa: ___ you check whether my name is on the list?
cards.backEn: Could you check whether my name is on the list?

cards.frontJa: ___ you tell me which line goes to the airport?
cards.backEn: Could you tell me which line goes to the airport?

cards.frontJa: Translate: こちらにお水をいただけますか。
cards.backEn: Could you bring us some water, please?

cards.frontJa: ___ you turn your microphone off when you're not speaking?
cards.backEn: Could you turn your microphone off when you're not speaking?

cards.frontJa: ___ you confirm the spelling of your surname?
cards.backEn: Could you confirm the spelling of your surname?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 写真を撮っていただけますか。
cards.backEn: Could you take a photo for us?

cards.frontJa: ___ you let me know if the schedule changes?
cards.backEn: Could you let me know if the schedule changes?

cards.frontJa: ___ you move your bag off the seat?
cards.backEn: Could you move your bag off the seat?

cards.frontJa: ___ you mind closing the window? It's a bit cold.
cards.backEn: Would you mind closing the window? It's a bit cold.

cards.frontJa: ___ you mind waiting for a few minutes?
cards.backEn: Would you mind waiting for a few minutes?

cards.frontJa: ___ if I sat here?
cards.backEn: Would you mind if I sat here?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 少し前に出ていただけますか。
cards.backEn: Would you mind stepping forward a little?

cards.frontJa: ___ you mind switching to the other line?
cards.backEn: Would you mind switching to the other line?

cards.frontJa: ___ if I used your charger?
cards.backEn: Would you mind if I used your charger?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 署名をこちらにお願いできますか。
cards.backEn: Would you mind signing here?

cards.frontJa: ___ you mind keeping your voice down?
cards.backEn: Would you mind keeping your voice down?

cards.frontJa: ___ if we started five minutes late?
cards.backEn: Would you mind if we started five minutes late?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 後ろのドアから降りていただけますか。
cards.backEn: Would you mind getting off through the rear door?

cards.frontJa: ___ you mind forwarding that email to me?
cards.backEn: Would you mind forwarding that email to me?

cards.frontJa: ___ if I took this chair?
cards.backEn: Would you mind if I took this chair?

cards.frontJa: I was ___ if you could review this tonight.
cards.backEn: I was wondering if you could review this tonight.

cards.frontJa: Would it be ___ to reschedule to Thursday?
cards.backEn: Would it be possible to reschedule to Thursday?

cards.frontJa: I'm sorry to ___, but could you help me with this form?
cards.backEn: I'm sorry to bother you, but could you help me with this form?

cards.frontJa: Translate: お忙しいところ恐れ入りますが、少しお時間よろしいでしょうか。
cards.backEn: Sorry to interrupt — do you have a moment?

cards.frontJa: Do you ___ if I join you for lunch?
cards.backEn: Do you mind if I join you for lunch?

cards.frontJa: Could I possibly ___ an extension until Monday?
cards.backEn: Could I possibly get an extension until Monday?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 差し支えなければ、お名前を教えていただけますか。
cards.backEn: If you don't mind, could I have your name?

cards.frontJa: I don't want to ___, but this doesn't look right.
cards.backEn: I don't want to sound rude, but this doesn't look right.

cards.frontJa: Would you be ___ to share the link again?
cards.backEn: Would you be willing to share the link again?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 恐縮ですが、もう一度説明していただけますか。
cards.backEn: I'm embarrassed to ask, but could you explain that again?

cards.frontJa: If it's not too much ___, could you double-check the total?
cards.backEn: If it's not too much trouble, could you double-check the total?

cards.frontJa: I would ___ appreciate a quick reply when you can.
cards.backEn: I would really appreciate a quick reply when you can.

cards.frontJa: This route is ___ than the one we took yesterday.
cards.backEn: This route is faster than the one we took yesterday.

cards.frontJa: The sequel was ___ interesting than the first film.
cards.backEn: The sequel was more interesting than the first film.

cards.frontJa: Living downtown is ___ expensive than I expected.
cards.backEn: Living downtown is more expensive than I expected.

cards.frontJa: Translate: この店の方が駅の近くにあります。
cards.backEn: This shop is closer to the station than that one.

cards.frontJa: She speaks more confidently ___ she did last year.
cards.backEn: She speaks more confidently than she did last year.

cards.frontJa: I like this laptop ___ it's lighter than my old one.
cards.backEn: I like this laptop because it's lighter than my old one.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 今日の方が昨日より暖かいです。
cards.backEn: Today is warmer than yesterday.

cards.frontJa: The new app is ___ complicated than the old version.
cards.backEn: The new app is less complicated than the old version.

cards.frontJa: He earns ___ than his colleague, but he works longer hours.
cards.backEn: He earns more than his colleague, but he works longer hours.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 飛行機の方がバスより速いです。
cards.backEn: The plane is faster than the bus.

cards.frontJa: This coffee tastes ___ than the one I usually buy.
cards.backEn: This coffee tastes better than the one I usually buy.

cards.frontJa: The meeting was ___ than we thought it would be.
cards.backEn: The meeting was shorter than we thought it would be.

cards.frontJa: That's ___ idea I've heard all week.
cards.backEn: That's the worst idea I've heard all week.

cards.frontJa: It was one of ___ concerts I've ever been to.
cards.backEn: It was one of the best concerts I've ever been to.

cards.frontJa: This is ___ option if you want to save money.
cards.backEn: This is the cheapest option if you want to save money.

cards.frontJa: Translate: この地域で一番静かな公園はどこですか。
cards.backEn: Where's the quietest park in this area?

cards.frontJa: She's ___ person on the team right now.
cards.backEn: She's the busiest person on the team right now.

cards.frontJa: I think Tuesday works ___ for everyone.
cards.backEn: I think Tuesday works best for everyone.

cards.frontJa: Translate: これは今月で最も重要な締め切りです。
cards.backEn: This is the most important deadline this month.

cards.frontJa: That's ___ polite way to say no.
cards.backEn: That's a polite way to say no.

cards.frontJa: Of the three, I found the middle one ___ useful.
cards.backEn: Of the three, I found the middle one the most useful.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その中で一番好きなのはどれですか。
cards.backEn: Which one do you like the most?

cards.frontJa: It's not ___ difficult topic we've covered.
cards.backEn: It's not the most difficult topic we've covered.

cards.frontJa: This hotel has ___ pool in town.
cards.backEn: This hotel has the nicest pool in town.

cards.frontJa: This bag is ___ heavy as it looks.
cards.backEn: This bag is as heavy as it looks.

cards.frontJa: The copy isn't ___ clear as the original.
cards.backEn: The copy isn't as clear as the original.

cards.frontJa: Try to arrive ___ early as you can.
cards.backEn: Try to arrive as early as you can.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は兄と同じくらい背が高いです。
cards.backEn: He's as tall as his older brother.

cards.frontJa: The second sample is ___ as expensive.
cards.backEn: The second sample is nearly twice as expensive.

cards.frontJa: She doesn't talk ___ much as she used to.
cards.backEn: She doesn't talk as much as she used to.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 想像していたほど難しくありませんでした。
cards.backEn: It wasn't as difficult as I imagined.

cards.frontJa: I'll stay ___ long as you need me.
cards.backEn: I'll stay as long as you need me.

cards.frontJa: The room was ___ big as we hoped.
cards.backEn: The room was as big as we hoped.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 昨日と同じくらい混んでいます。
cards.backEn: It's as crowded as it was yesterday.

cards.frontJa: He's not ___ patient as his manager.
cards.backEn: He's not as patient as his manager.

cards.frontJa: Please pack ___ carefully as you can.
cards.backEn: Please pack as carefully as you can.

cards.frontJa: I have ___ with my reservation — it's under the wrong name.
cards.backEn: I have an issue with my reservation — it's under the wrong name.

cards.frontJa: Something is ___ with the Wi‑Fi; I can't connect.
cards.backEn: Something is wrong with the Wi‑Fi; I can't connect.

cards.frontJa: I'm having ___ getting the file to upload.
cards.backEn: I'm having trouble getting the file to upload.

cards.frontJa: Translate: お会計に誤りがあるようです。
cards.backEn: There seems to be a mistake on the bill.

cards.frontJa: The screen keeps ___ — could someone take a look?
cards.backEn: The screen keeps freezing — could someone take a look?

cards.frontJa: This isn't ___ I ordered.
cards.backEn: This isn't what I ordered.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 部屋のエアコンが動きません。
cards.backEn: The air conditioner in my room isn't working.

cards.frontJa: I'd like to ___ a complaint about the noise last night.
cards.backEn: I'd like to make a complaint about the noise last night.

cards.frontJa: We're running ___ a small problem with the delivery.
cards.backEn: We're running into a small problem with the delivery.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 鍵が部屋で開きません。
cards.backEn: The key won't open the door to my room.

cards.frontJa: Could you help me ___ this out?
cards.backEn: Could you help me sort this out?

cards.frontJa: I'm not sure what's ___, but the alarm won't stop.
cards.backEn: I'm not sure what's going on, but the alarm won't stop.

cards.frontJa: Let me ___ what we can do about this.
cards.backEn: Let me see what we can do about this.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ this to the maintenance team right away.
cards.backEn: I'll report this to the maintenance team right away.

cards.frontJa: We can ___ you a replacement by tomorrow.
cards.backEn: We can send you a replacement by tomorrow.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 申し訳ございません。すぐに確認します。
cards.backEn: I'm sorry — I'll check that right away.

cards.frontJa: If that doesn't work, ___ try restarting the router.
cards.backEn: If that doesn't work, let's try restarting the router.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ you posted as soon as I hear back.
cards.backEn: I'll keep you posted as soon as I hear back.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 別の部屋に移していただけますか。
cards.backEn: Could we move to a different room?

cards.frontJa: Would a ___ be okay instead?
cards.backEn: Would a refund be okay instead?

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ a note so it doesn't happen again.
cards.backEn: I'll leave a note so it doesn't happen again.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 再発防止のため、記録を残します。
cards.backEn: I'll log this so we can prevent it from happening again.

cards.frontJa: Can we ___ on this tomorrow morning?
cards.backEn: Can we follow up on this tomorrow morning?

cards.frontJa: Thanks for ___ — I appreciate your patience.
cards.backEn: Thanks for letting us know — I appreciate your patience.

cards.frontJa: Sorry, I need to ___ — I have another call.
cards.backEn: Sorry, I need to go — I have another call.

cards.frontJa: Could we ___ the next slide?
cards.backEn: Could we move to the next slide?

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ the action items at the end.
cards.backEn: I'll summarize the action items at the end.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 本日の議題を確認してもよろしいでしょうか。
cards.backEn: Shall we go over today's agenda?

cards.frontJa: ___ we take a five-minute break?
cards.backEn: Can we take a five-minute break?

cards.frontJa: I'm not sure I ___ that last point — could you clarify?
cards.backEn: I'm not sure I followed that last point — could you clarify?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 次回の会議は金曜の午後でよいですか。
cards.backEn: Is Friday afternoon okay for the next meeting?

cards.frontJa: Let me ___ that back to you to check I understood.
cards.backEn: Let me feed that back to you to check I understood.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ the minutes after the call.
cards.backEn: I'll circulate the minutes after the call.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 発言したい方は挙手をお願いします。
cards.backEn: Please raise your hand if you'd like to speak.

cards.frontJa: We're slightly ___ schedule, so let's stay focused.
cards.backEn: We're slightly behind schedule, so let's stay focused.

cards.frontJa: Could everyone ___ their mics on mute unless speaking?
cards.backEn: Could everyone keep their mics on mute unless speaking?

cards.frontJa: I'm writing to ___ about tomorrow's deadline.
cards.backEn: I'm writing to follow up about tomorrow's deadline.

cards.frontJa: Please find the report ___ .
cards.backEn: Please find the report attached.

cards.frontJa: Let me know if you need any ___ information.
cards.backEn: Let me know if you need any further information.

cards.frontJa: Translate: ご確認のほどよろしくお願いいたします。
cards.backEn: Thank you in advance for your review.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ you a calendar invite this afternoon.
cards.backEn: I'll send you a calendar invite this afternoon.

cards.frontJa: Could you ___ me in copy on that thread?
cards.backEn: Could you add me in copy on that thread?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 返信が遅れて申し訳ありません。
cards.backEn: Sorry for the delayed reply.

cards.frontJa: I'm ___ of the office until Thursday.
cards.backEn: I'm out of the office until Thursday.

cards.frontJa: Just a quick ___ to confirm we received your payment.
cards.backEn: Just a quick note to confirm we received your payment.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 添付ファイルが開けないのですが。
cards.backEn: I'm having trouble opening the attachment.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ back to you by end of day.
cards.backEn: I'll get back to you by end of day.

cards.frontJa: Thanks ___ your patience while we investigate.
cards.backEn: Thanks for your patience while we investigate.

cards.frontJa: We're ___ against a tight deadline this week.
cards.backEn: We're up against a tight deadline this week.

cards.frontJa: Could we ___ the due date by one day?
cards.backEn: Could we extend the due date by one day?

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ this task first thing tomorrow.
cards.backEn: I'll tackle this task first thing tomorrow.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 最優先で対応してください。
cards.backEn: Please treat this as top priority.

cards.frontJa: If we can't finish today, we should ___ stakeholders early.
cards.backEn: If we can't finish today, we should flag stakeholders early.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ what must be done today vs. next week.
cards.backEn: Let's break down what must be done today vs. next week.

cards.frontJa: Translate: スケジュールに余裕はありますか。
cards.backEn: Do we have any slack in the schedule?

cards.frontJa: I'm blocked ___ I get approval from finance.
cards.backEn: I'm blocked until I get approval from finance.

cards.frontJa: Can we ___ resources for the launch next month?
cards.backEn: Can we allocate resources for the launch next month?

cards.frontJa: Translate: この部分は後回しにしても大丈夫ですか。
cards.backEn: Is it okay to deprioritize this part?

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ you a realistic timeline by noon.
cards.backEn: I'll give you a realistic timeline by noon.

cards.frontJa: We need to ___ expectations if the scope grows.
cards.backEn: We need to manage expectations if the scope grows.

cards.frontJa: ___, log in with your work email.
cards.backEn: First, log in with your work email.

cards.frontJa: ___, open the settings tab.
cards.backEn: Then, open the settings tab.

cards.frontJa: ___, click save and restart the app.
cards.backEn: Finally, click save and restart the app.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 最初に袋から中身を全部出してください。
cards.backEn: First, take everything out of the bag.

cards.frontJa: After that, you should ___ a backup copy.
cards.backEn: After that, you should make a backup copy.

cards.frontJa: Before you start, ___ sure the device is charged.
cards.backEn: Before you start, make sure the device is charged.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 次に、電源ボタンを長押しします。
cards.backEn: Next, hold the power button down.

cards.frontJa: Once the light turns green, you can ___ the cable.
cards.backEn: Once the light turns green, you can unplug the cable.

cards.frontJa: If anything looks odd, ___ the process and call us.
cards.backEn: If anything looks odd, stop the process and call us.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 最後に、蓋をしっかり閉めてください。
cards.backEn: Lastly, close the lid tightly.

cards.frontJa: The next ___ is to verify your identity.
cards.backEn: The next step is to verify your identity.

cards.frontJa: At this point, you ___ wait about two minutes.
cards.backEn: At this point, you should wait about two minutes.

cards.frontJa: ___, rinse the rice until the water runs clear.
cards.backEn: First, rinse the rice until the water runs clear.

cards.frontJa: ___, add water up to the line inside the pot.
cards.backEn: Next, add water up to the line inside the pot.

cards.frontJa: ___, let it rest for ten minutes before opening.
cards.backEn: Finally, let it rest for ten minutes before opening.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その後、よく混ぜてください。
cards.backEn: After that, mix it well.

cards.frontJa: When you're done, ___ the tools in the sink.
cards.backEn: When you're done, leave the tools in the sink.

cards.frontJa: Until the beep sounds, don't ___ the lid.
cards.backEn: Until the beep sounds, don't open the lid.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 準備ができたら知らせてください。
cards.backEn: Let me know when you're ready.

cards.frontJa: Meanwhile, ___ the vegetables on the side.
cards.backEn: Meanwhile, chop the vegetables on the side.

cards.frontJa: By the time it boils, the sauce ___ be ready.
cards.backEn: By the time it boils, the sauce should be ready.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 問題があれば途中で止めてください。
cards.backEn: If there's a problem, stop halfway.

cards.frontJa: Afterward, we can ___ the results together.
cards.backEn: Afterward, we can review the results together.

cards.frontJa: In the meantime, feel ___ to grab coffee.
cards.backEn: In the meantime, feel free to grab coffee.

cards.frontJa: I went to Kyoto last spring, and it ___ amazing.
cards.backEn: I went to Kyoto last spring, and it was amazing.

cards.frontJa: We tried that new café, but it ___ too crowded.
cards.backEn: We tried that new café, but it was too crowded.

cards.frontJa: I ___ there once on business.
cards.backEn: I've been there once on business.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 週末に友達とキャンプに行きました。
cards.backEn: I went camping with friends over the weekend.

cards.frontJa: It was the first time I ___ sushi in Japan.
cards.backEn: It was the first time I ate sushi in Japan.

cards.frontJa: After the concert, we ___ for a walk by the river.
cards.backEn: After the concert, we went for a walk by the river.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 子供の頃、毎年海に行っていました。
cards.backEn: When I was a child, we went to the beach every year.

cards.frontJa: I didn't expect the hike to be ___ tiring.
cards.backEn: I didn't expect the hike to be this tiring.

cards.frontJa: Last year I ___ a short course online.
cards.backEn: Last year I took a short course online.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その時、とても緊張していました。
cards.backEn: At that time, I was really nervous.

cards.frontJa: We got ___ late because of traffic.
cards.backEn: We got home late because of traffic.

cards.frontJa: I ___ a lot of photos, but I lost my phone.
cards.backEn: I took a lot of photos, but I lost my phone.

cards.frontJa: When I arrived, the store ___ already closed.
cards.backEn: When I arrived, the store was already closed.

cards.frontJa: While we were waiting, it ___ to rain.
cards.backEn: While we were waiting, it started to rain.

cards.frontJa: After I finished work, I ___ straight home.
cards.backEn: After I finished work, I went straight home.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 会議が終わった後、食事に行きました。
cards.backEn: After the meeting, we went out for dinner.

cards.frontJa: I was about to leave ___ someone called my name.
cards.backEn: I was about to leave when someone called my name.

cards.frontJa: By the time we got there, the show ___ sold out.
cards.backEn: By the time we got there, the show was sold out.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その日はとても忙しい一日でした。
cards.backEn: It was a really busy day.

cards.frontJa: I ___ never seen snow before that trip.
cards.backEn: I had never seen snow before that trip.

cards.frontJa: We ___ lost for an hour near the station.
cards.backEn: We got lost for an hour near the station.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 楽しかったですが、疲れました。
cards.backEn: It was fun, but I was exhausted.

cards.frontJa: I ___ I'd booked a window seat.
cards.backEn: I wish I'd booked a window seat.

cards.frontJa: In the end, we ___ a taxi because we were late.
cards.backEn: In the end, we took a taxi because we were late.

cards.frontJa: ___ we left the house, we realized we'd forgotten tickets.
cards.backEn: As soon as we left the house, we realized we'd forgotten the tickets.

cards.frontJa: ___ the train arrived, everyone rushed to the doors.
cards.backEn: When the train arrived, everyone rushed to the doors.

cards.frontJa: ___ that, we decided to walk instead.
cards.backEn: After that, we decided to walk instead.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 突然、雨が降り始めました。
cards.backEn: Suddenly, it started to rain.

cards.frontJa: ___ we were chatting, the lights went out.
cards.backEn: While we were chatting, the lights went out.

cards.frontJa: ___ the end, everything worked out fine.
cards.backEn: In the end, everything worked out fine.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 幸いなことに、誰も怪我をしませんでした。
cards.backEn: Luckily, nobody was hurt.

cards.frontJa: ___ a result, we missed the opening act.
cards.backEn: As a result, we missed the opening act.

cards.frontJa: ___ first I was nervous, but then I relaxed.
cards.backEn: At first I was nervous, but then I relaxed.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 結局、自分でやってみることにしました。
cards.backEn: Eventually, I decided to try it myself.

cards.frontJa: ___ moment we sat down, the show began.
cards.backEn: The moment we sat down, the show began.

cards.frontJa: ___ hearing the news, she called her parents.
cards.backEn: On hearing the news, she called her parents.

cards.frontJa: You ___ get some rest — you sound exhausted.
cards.backEn: You should get some rest — you sound exhausted.

cards.frontJa: He ought to ___ a doctor about that cough.
cards.backEn: He ought to see a doctor about that cough.

cards.frontJa: We should probably ___ earlier if we want good seats.
cards.backEn: We should probably leave earlier if we want good seats.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その前にもう一度確認した方がいいです。
cards.backEn: You should double-check that beforehand.

cards.frontJa: You shouldn't ___ passwords in chat.
cards.backEn: You shouldn't share passwords in chat.

cards.frontJa: I think you ___ talk to HR about the schedule.
cards.backEn: I think you should talk to HR about the schedule.

cards.frontJa: Translate: そんなに無理をしない方がいいですよ。
cards.backEn: You ought not to push yourself so hard.

cards.frontJa: They should ___ notified us about the closure.
cards.backEn: They should have notified us about the closure.

cards.frontJa: You might want to ___ a jacket — it's cold outside.
cards.backEn: You might want to bring a jacket — it's cold outside.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 正直に言った方がいいと思います。
cards.backEn: I think you should be honest about it.

cards.frontJa: We ought to ___ thank the host before we leave.
cards.backEn: We ought to thank the host before we leave.

cards.frontJa: If I were you, I'd ___ a backup plan.
cards.backEn: If I were you, I'd keep a backup plan.

decks.titleJa: 助言②（Why don't you / How about）

cards.frontJa: ___ you try the vegetarian option?
cards.backEn: Why don't you try the vegetarian option?

cards.frontJa: ___ meeting on Zoom instead of in person?
cards.backEn: How about meeting on Zoom instead of in person?

cards.frontJa: ___ we split the bill evenly?
cards.backEn: Why don't we split the bill evenly?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 一度相談してみたらどうですか。
cards.backEn: How about talking to them once?

cards.frontJa: ___ taking a short walk before dinner?
cards.backEn: How about taking a short walk before dinner?

cards.frontJa: ___ you ask at the information desk?
cards.backEn: Why don't you ask at the information desk?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 明日の午後はどうですか。
cards.backEn: How about tomorrow afternoon?

cards.frontJa: ___ we grab coffee after this?
cards.backEn: Shall we grab coffee after this?

cards.frontJa: Maybe you could ___ a day off social media.
cards.backEn: Maybe you could take a day off social media.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 別の店を試してみませんか。
cards.backEn: Why not try another shop?

cards.frontJa: I'd suggest ___ the train if traffic is bad.
cards.backEn: I'd suggest taking the train if traffic is bad.

cards.frontJa: Have you thought ___ joining the weekend workshop?
cards.backEn: Have you thought about joining the weekend workshop?

cards.frontJa: One option ___ be to postpone the launch.
cards.backEn: One option might be to postpone the launch.

cards.frontJa: It ___ be worth comparing prices online.
cards.backEn: It might be worth comparing prices online.

cards.frontJa: I'd ___ keeping a copy on a USB drive.
cards.backEn: I'd recommend keeping a copy on a USB drive.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 個人的には、もう少し待つべきだと思います。
cards.backEn: Personally, I think we should wait a bit longer.

cards.frontJa: If you ask me, we ___ focus on quality first.
cards.backEn: If you ask me, we should focus on quality first.

cards.frontJa: My ___ would be to start with the basics.
cards.backEn: My advice would be to start with the basics.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 試しにやってみる価値はあると思います。
cards.backEn: I think it's worth giving it a try.

cards.frontJa: You could always ___ for help if you get stuck.
cards.backEn: You could always ask for help if you get stuck.

cards.frontJa: Perhaps we should ___ the scope smaller.
cards.backEn: Perhaps we should keep the scope smaller.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 急がず、一つずつ進めましょう。
cards.backEn: Let's take it step by step without rushing.

cards.frontJa: I wouldn't ___ skipping the safety check.
cards.backEn: I wouldn't recommend skipping the safety check.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ it simple for the first version.
cards.backEn: Let's keep it simple for the first version.

cards.frontJa: We need a clear ___ before we spend more money.
cards.backEn: We need a clear plan before we spend more money.

cards.frontJa: That was a tough ___, but I'm glad we made it.
cards.backEn: That was a tough decision, but I'm glad we made it.

cards.frontJa: The ___ of the project exceeded our expectations.
cards.backEn: The result of the project exceeded our expectations.

cards.frontJa: Translate: そのアイデアは実現可能だと思います。
cards.backEn: I think that idea is feasible.

cards.frontJa: She explained the ___ behind the change.
cards.backEn: She explained the reason behind the change.

cards.frontJa: Let's discuss the ___ and cons calmly.
cards.backEn: Let's discuss the pros and cons calmly.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 結果を踏まえて方針を変えましょう。
cards.backEn: Let's adjust our approach based on the outcome.

cards.frontJa: We don't have enough ___ to hire two people.
cards.backEn: We don't have enough budget to hire two people.

cards.frontJa: His ___ attitude helped the team stay calm.
cards.backEn: His positive attitude helped the team stay calm.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 優先順位をはっきりさせましょう。
cards.backEn: Let's clarify our priorities.

cards.frontJa: The main ___ is whether we can meet the deadline.
cards.backEn: The main issue is whether we can meet the deadline.

cards.frontJa: We reached ___ after a long discussion.
cards.backEn: We reached an agreement after a long discussion.

cards.frontJa: There's been a change ___ schedule.
cards.backEn: There's been a change of schedule.

cards.frontJa: We need your ___ on the new policy.
cards.backEn: We need your feedback on the new policy.

cards.frontJa: The team showed great ___ under pressure.
cards.backEn: The team showed great cooperation under pressure.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 進捗を共有していただけますか。
cards.backEn: Could you share the progress with us?

cards.frontJa: Let's set realistic ___ for this quarter.
cards.backEn: Let's set realistic goals for this quarter.

cards.frontJa: We should document the ___ so nothing is forgotten.
cards.backEn: We should document the process so nothing is forgotten.

cards.frontJa: Translate: リスクを最小限に抑えましょう。
cards.backEn: Let's minimize the risk.

cards.frontJa: The ___ of this tool is to save time.
cards.backEn: The purpose of this tool is to save time.

cards.frontJa: We learned a valuable ___ from that mistake.
cards.backEn: We learned a valuable lesson from that mistake.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 期待値をそろえましょう。
cards.backEn: Let's align our expectations.

cards.frontJa: There's no simple ___ to this problem.
cards.backEn: There's no simple solution to this problem.

cards.frontJa: We need more ___ before we decide.
cards.backEn: We need more information before we decide.

cards.frontJa: ___ you hear me okay?
cards.backEn: Can you hear me okay?

cards.frontJa: Sorry, you're ___ — could you say that again?
cards.backEn: Sorry, you're breaking up — could you say that again?

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ it to you by email right after this call.
cards.backEn: I'll send it to you by email right after this call.

cards.frontJa: Translate: お電話ありがとうございます。田中と申します。
cards.backEn: Thank you for calling. This is Tanaka speaking.

cards.frontJa: Could I speak to someone ___ billing?
cards.backEn: Could I speak to someone in billing?

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ you through to the right department.
cards.backEn: I'll transfer you through to the right department.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 今ちょっと手が離せないので、後でかけ直します。
cards.backEn: I can't talk right now — I'll call you back later.

cards.frontJa: The line went ___ for a second.
cards.backEn: The line went dead for a second.

cards.frontJa: Let me ___ you back in five minutes.
cards.backEn: Let me call you back in five minutes.

cards.frontJa: Translate: お待ちください。つなぎます。
cards.backEn: Please hold — I'll connect you.

cards.frontJa: I didn't ___ your name — could you spell it?
cards.backEn: I didn't catch your name — could you spell it?

cards.frontJa: We're having ___ issues with the audio today.
cards.backEn: We're having technical issues with the audio today.

decks.titleJa: オンライン会議・チャット

cards.frontJa: I think you're still ___ — we can't see your slides.
cards.backEn: I think you're still muted — we can't see your slides.

cards.frontJa: Could you ___ your camera on?
cards.backEn: Could you turn your camera on?

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ the link in the chat.
cards.backEn: I'll paste the link in the chat.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 画面共有をお願いできますか。
cards.backEn: Could you share your screen?

cards.frontJa: Sorry, I had to ___ off video — bad signal.
cards.backEn: Sorry, I had to turn off video — bad signal.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ the recording for people who missed it.
cards.backEn: Let's save the recording for people who missed it.

cards.frontJa: Translate: チャットに質問を書いてください。
cards.backEn: Please type your question in the chat.

cards.frontJa: I'll follow up ___ the thread after the call.
cards.backEn: I'll follow up in the thread after the call.

cards.frontJa: Can everyone see the ___ I'm sharing?
cards.backEn: Can everyone see the document I'm sharing?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 回線が不安定なので、音声のみで参加します。
cards.backEn: My connection is unstable, so I'll join audio only.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ the meeting now — thanks everyone.
cards.backEn: I'll leave the meeting now — thanks everyone.

cards.frontJa: Could you ___ that message one more time?
cards.backEn: Could you resend that message one more time?

cards.frontJa: Where do I ___ in for an international flight?
cards.backEn: Where do I check in for an international flight?

cards.frontJa: My flight was ___ — what are my options?
cards.backEn: My flight was canceled — what are my options?

cards.frontJa: Is this the ___ gate for the flight to Seattle?
cards.backEn: Is this the correct gate for the flight to Seattle?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 手荷物はどこで受け取りますか。
cards.backEn: Where do I pick up my luggage?

cards.frontJa: How early should I ___ at the airport?
cards.backEn: How early should I arrive at the airport?

cards.frontJa: I'd like an ___ seat, please.
cards.backEn: I'd like an aisle seat, please.

cards.frontJa: Translate: このバスは空港に行きますか。
cards.backEn: Does this bus go to the airport?

cards.frontJa: Could you help me ___ this ticket machine?
cards.backEn: Could you help me with this ticket machine?

cards.frontJa: I ___ my passport at the hotel.
cards.backEn: I left my passport at the hotel.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 乗り換えはどの番線ですか。
cards.backEn: Which platform is my connection from?

cards.frontJa: The security line is ___ than I expected.
cards.backEn: The security line is longer than I expected.

cards.frontJa: We need to ___ terminals before the next flight.
cards.backEn: We need to change terminals before the next flight.

cards.frontJa: I have a reservation ___ Tanaka.
cards.backEn: I have a reservation under Tanaka.

cards.frontJa: Could we ___ a late checkout?
cards.backEn: Could we get a late checkout?

cards.frontJa: Is breakfast ___ in the rate?
cards.backEn: Is breakfast included in the rate?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 駅まで歩いてどのくらいですか。
cards.backEn: How far is it to walk to the station?

cards.frontJa: ___ me, how do I get to the museum from here?
cards.backEn: Excuse me, how do I get to the museum from here?

cards.frontJa: Is it ___ to walk at night in this area?
cards.backEn: Is it safe to walk at night in this area?

cards.frontJa: Translate: この道をまっすぐ行って、二つ目を右です。
cards.backEn: Go straight on this road and turn right at the second light.

cards.frontJa: Turn ___ at the corner and you'll see the sign.
cards.backEn: Turn left at the corner and you'll see the sign.

cards.frontJa: It's about a ten-minute ___ from here.
cards.backEn: It's about a ten-minute walk from here.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 地図アプリではここが目的地と違います。
cards.backEn: The map app says this isn't the right place.

cards.frontJa: Could you ___ a taxi for two people?
cards.backEn: Could you call a taxi for two people?

cards.frontJa: I think we're ___ — we passed this shop already.
cards.backEn: I think we're lost — we passed this shop already.

items.promptJa: 「If it rains tomorrow, I will stay home.」に最も近い文は？
items.promptEn: Which sentence is closest to a first conditional about rain and staying home?
options: If it will rain tomorrow, I stay home.
If it rains tomorrow, I will stay home.
If it rained tomorrow, I will stay home.
If it rains tomorrow, I would stay home.

items.promptJa: 丁寧な依頼として自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which is a natural polite request?
options: Could you close the window, please?

items.promptJa: 比較として正しいのは？
options: This book is more better than that one.
This book is better than that one.
This book is gooder than that one.
This book is more good than that one.

items.promptJa: 問題を伝える表現として自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which line naturally reports a problem?
options: I have wrong with my room.
Something is wrong with my room.

items.promptJa: 会議で使える表現はどれ？
items.promptEn: Which phrase fits a work meeting?
options: Could we move to the next slide?

items.promptJa: 手順の並びとして自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which sequence sounds natural for instructions?
options: First log in, then open settings, finally save.

items.promptJa: 過去の体験を述べる文として自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which sentence naturally describes a past experience?
options: I go to Kyoto and it amazing.
I went to Kyoto, and it was amazing.

items.promptJa: 助言として自然なのは？
options: You must to see a doctor.

items.promptJa: 電話で聞こえにくいとき、自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which line is natural on a bad phone line?

items.promptJa: 空港でチェックインを尋ねる表現は？
items.promptEn: Which asks where to check in at the airport?
options: Where do I check in for an international flight?

items.promptJa: 「Would you mind closing the window?」に近い丁寧さは？
options: Would you mind closing the window?

items.promptJa: 抽象名詞の使い方として自然なのは？
options: We need a clear idea before a decision.
We need clear idea before decision without article.
We need idea clear a decision.
